text:
Rare earth elements, including neodymium, play an important role in the global economy. This paper explores the use and management of REE in the world economy and evaluates the future trends of global REE demand. A number of countries around the world have made various solemn promises to lower carbon emissions and to reduce their pollutants and this, along with an emerging digital economy has increased the need for neodymium-iron-boron (NdFeB) magnets, widely used in the EV motor of EVs, in wind turbines and other products such as intelligent household appliances etc. This paper analyzed the current situation of REE global demand, production and prediction showed that REE demand from the magnet industry will account for over 70% of global REE demand by 2030 and is likely to lead to a tightened balance between global REE supply and demand.

Due to the clustering of primary resources and the strategic nature of these materials, it is becoming increasingly necessary to apply principles of a circular economy. There are various opportunities for secondary resource production through advanced recycling technologies such as the reuse of decommissioned hard disk drives and air conditioner compressors. In addition, global initiatives and projects are unfolding e.g. new sorting facilities in Poland have been opened and European alliances for the secure supply of rare earths and other strategic materials have been founded. In summary, it can be concluded that neodymium and thus the recycling of neodymium needs to be sustainably managed on a global level, taking into account the new and evolving recycling techniques in combination with international co-operations in order to ensure world wide economic stability in the field of high technology products and renewable energy technologies.
